claim: Factorio requires mining approximately 1 million raw resources to complete a rocket launch, while Minecraft requires only ~200 resources for equivalent progression, giving Factorio orders of magnitude more complexity
actionable: Use Factorio as a benchmark environment when evaluating LLM agent scalability, as it provides a more gradual complexity curve than existing game-based evals
source_title: Factorio Learning Environment: the ultimate Game Agent Eval

claim: Using low-level action APIs (move left/right primitives) prevents LLMs from leveraging their pre-training; Python code synthesis approach is better for preserving learned abstractions
actionable: When designing agent interfaces, expose high-level action abstractions (e.g., 'place entity next to another') via Python APIs rather than low-level movement commands to preserve LLM pre-training value
source_title: Factorio Learning Environment: the ultimate Game Agent Eval

claim: DeepSeek performs well on structured lab-play tasks (goal-oriented, fixed targets) but fails on open-play (unbounded goal-setting), often defaulting to simple repetitive actions like creating identical entities
actionable: Design multi-phase agent evaluations that test both constrained task execution and autonomous goal formation; use open-play benchmarks to expose myopic objective-setting
source_title: Factorio Learning Environment: the ultimate Game Agent Eval

claim: Claude (Sonnet 3.5) uses fire-and-forget coding style while GPT-4 uses defensive programming with assertions, reflecting fundamentally different coding styles across model families
actionable: When designing agent scaffolding, adapt failure recovery patterns to match the coding style of the underlying model (assertion-heavy for GPT-4, lenient for Claude)
source_title: Factorio Learning Environment: the ultimate Game Agent Eval

claim: Reasoning models (e.g., o1) do not outperform smaller models on Factorio eval when the environment already requires extended reasoning; 'reasoning on reasoning' eliminates performance gains
actionable: When evaluating reasoning models, use benchmarks that don't already enforce multi-step reasoning loops; reasoning models show largest gains on single-step complex reasoning tasks
source_title: Factorio Learning Environment: the ultimate Game Agent Eval

claim: Adding raw screenshot vision to Factorio eval made agents hallucinate non-existent entities and perform worse; simple geometric renderers that agents can configure perform better
actionable: When adding vision to structured environments, use simplified geometric representations agents can control rather than raw screenshots, as this reduces hallucination and improves interpretability
source_title: Factorio Learning Environment: the ultimate Game Agent Eval

claim: Goal content integrity (reluctance to change objectives once set) is hypothesized as an emergent instrumental convergence behavior that may surface when training agents on unbounded objectives
actionable: Design training setups where agents must demonstrate goal malleability; treat unexpected goal commitment as a safety signal requiring investigation
source_title: Factorio Learning Environment: the ultimate Game Agent Eval

claim: Current untrained models are ~100x slower than human expert speedrunners in Factorio (8+ hours vs 80 minutes), but this gap may shrink with training on Factorio-specific objectives
actionable: Use speedrun time benchmarks as a secondary eval metric alongside production efficiency to track agent learning progress
source_title: Factorio Learning Environment: the ultimate Game Agent Eval
